reasoning:
"One Battle After Another" has achieved a nearly unprecedented awards season sweep, winning the DGA, PGA, BAFTA, Golden Globe, and the major critics clusters (NBR, NYFCC, LAFCA). Winning the PGA (which uses the Academy's preferential ballot) combined with the DGA typically establishes a >85% win rate for Best Picture. Betting odds just ahead of the PGA mirrored this, putting the film at 1/4 (implying ~80%).

However, the Best Picture race is not fully sealed because its primary competitor, "Sinners," is a historic juggernaut in its own right: it set an all-time record for Oscar nominations (implying unanimous support across branches) and just won the SAG Award for Outstanding Performance by a Cast. Because actors form the largest voting bloc in the Academy, a SAG Ensemble win from the nomination leader is the exact recipe for a preferential ballot upset (reminiscent of 'Parasite' beating '1917' or 'Crash' beating 'Brokeback Mountain'). Given the PGA, DGA, and BAFTA wins, "One Battle After Another" remains a very solid favorite, but it accounts for an upset risk. An 82% forecast reflects its dominant front-runner status alongside a tangible path to victory for its rival.
